text:
Once upon a time there was a young girl named Ellie. Every weekend Ellie would wander through her town's park humming a silly tune. She loved collecting acorns. They were her favorite things. One sunny Saturday she discovered something that she'd never seen before: an ancient oak tree full of mysterious nut-bearing acorns.

She gathered them all up. Each morning she sat on a stump talking to them. "Dear ol' walnut," she'd say. "You're staying right here, no running away."

One squirrel replied, "It sounds boring out there talking to trees." Ellie giggled.

One day a wise old owl hooted. "Oh hi there. I'm old owl Oakie-nutterosis.”

“We talk all the time!” Ellie said eagerly. “How long?"

“Forever if you’re good. Just remember squirrels aren’t supposed to collect all that mastodon meat.”

She nodded. One autumn evening she talked to an old nut who'd saved a nut for years
